item.title: Non-vote
item.content:encoded: Definitely had some high-quality atmosphere in the first half… but I feel like it switches to something different in the second half. At first, it is a mysterious horror story, but at around the second addendum, it becomes a pattern screamer style sci-fi story that answers questions rather than poses questions like a horror story would. And while I like pattern screamer style stories and mysterious horror stories that pose a thousand questions and both half are written well, this article would be a lot better if you stuck to one or the other or attempted to consistently blend them. No vote.
This is amazing and kinda sad that my version of the picture didnt work out for you would have loved to see it but ehh life goes on +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the first half of the article a lot; the atmosphere is built up nicely, especially the introduction of the broadcast paired with the images. Maybe it's just me but I feel that the ending wasn't as satisfying as it could have been. Totally my opinion though, and I'm glad this is doing well because I really do like the vibe this article gives off.

The straight-up horror of the description and first addendum is really well done. The last two addenda elevate the article beyond this, into a warped and messed up sci-fi that reminds me of Superman's origin story. The broadcast image and transcript are particular highlights.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this, the concept is executed well with a focus on unsettling horror…

I do suggest that you cut the last addendum, and retool the interview to have a punchier ending. I don’t think the last addendum does much, as we’ve already got the sense that there’s alt universes at play… but also because it doesn’t explain much.

The white text is very good!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I admit I'm not the quickest in understanding concepts and messages and ideas immediately, but once I got what was being stated, I loved it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was good, however, I suggest you remove or rewrite Addendum.3651.3:. In the interview it's clear what you are talking about, kids from a different universe being sent here to live. The last addendum just reiterates that concept.
The atmosphere presented here is really creepy and well done.
Ah, glad to see this up! I love this, it feels almost like a pastarasta SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better than when I last looked over this. The atmosphere of the MTF log has been intensely concentrated on the horror, and the horror is effective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's hidden text in this article. Make sure to read it if you haven't.

Thanks to the unreasonably large amount of people who looked over this:

wizzblizz, placeholder, plaguepjp, pedagon, cerastes, ratking, voidlady, dysadron, stormbreath, and brewsterion.

i highly suggest checking out Hitogata, the lost media wiki page this article was loosely inspired by.

THE chalk image was made by WizzBlizz, send them a DM if you want something photoshopped
